scriptId: cs_v1_EARLY_L40_Excessive phone or social media use
historyId: h_v1_EARLY_L40_Excessive phone or social media use

goal: Reduce phone and social media usage.
constraints: Weekdays: keep phone in another room from 7:15 to 8:45.
Social-media usage limited to no more than 90 minutes per week, starting March 1, 2026.
No app deletion; prefer gradual, configurable changes.
Limit or silence push notifications and hide badge counts.
Disable autoplay features in apps to reduce ongoing session length.
Experiment with grayscale for 1-2 weeks as a low-effort nudge and monitor nightly metrics (daily screen time and pickups).
decisions: Implement grayscale testing for 1-2 weeks; monitor nightly metrics; consider pairing with silenced notifications/badges or scheduled Focus blocks for greater effect.

Add motive-based intervention plan: for social-driven usage reduce notification/badge triggers and implement focused reply-first windows; for news-driven usage schedule 1-2 brief checks, mute breaking-news alerts, and use curated summaries.
entities: single-purpose browsing (log out of social apps)
check-in windows (e.g., 15 minutes mid-morning, 20 minutes after dinner)
Pause ritual (2–3 minutes) before opening apps
Motive categories: social connection vs news/information
Trigger types by motive: interpersonal cues vs uncertainty-driven checks
Turn off badge counts / notification dots
Use Focus / Do Not Disturb with exceptions for people, not apps
Set app time limits / Screen Time / Digital Wellbeing limits (with block at end of limit)
Schedule Downtime / Bedtime Modes for evenings
Move apps off the main home screen (use folders or App Library)
Turn on grayscale or reduce color saturation
Disable background app refresh for social/feed apps
Disable 'raise to wake' / reduce accidental wake triggers
Use focused home screen pages during work/focus times (iOS Focus pages / Android work profile or minimalist launcher)
open: Plan how to divide and track 90 minutes of social-media use per week with partner, starting March 1, 2026.
Provide a plan with clear short-term milestones compatible with a busy life.
Incorporate a simple nightly checks routine (2–5 minutes) to monitor progress without deep data analysis.
Implement an evening doomscrolling strategy: choose a calendar-friendly option for your evenings (A-D or custom), set a timer, and enable Bedtime Focus.
Set up an evening Focus rule to silence feed notifications while keeping messages/priority chats accessible.
Add a 2–3 minute pause ritual before opening social apps and a 'reply-first' rule for evening checks.
Optionally pilot grayscale testing in the evening as a nudge and monitor impact.
Create motive-based lever plan and configure settings for social-driven vs news-driven usage.

Assess real-world effectiveness of app time limits and identify additional configurations or supports to improve adherence (e.g., stricter blocking, cross-device coverage, reducing triggers, accountability).
Evaluate and pilot a device-free meetings policy to reduce in-meeting phone use and assess impact on overall phone time.
